title: Mandalorian Civil War (True Mandalorians)
content:
{{Top|canon=Mandalorian Civil Wars}} {{Otheruses|Mandalorian civil war|more=Civil war}} {{Citation}} {{War |prev=*[[Bitthævrian War]] *[[Xanatos crisis]] |conc=*[[Melida/Daan Civil War]] *[[Telosian Civil War]] *[[Summertime War]] *[[Arkanian Revolution]] *[[Sepan Civil War]] *[[Young–Elders War]] *[[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]] *[[Kol Huro Unrest]] |next=[[Huk War]] |image=[[File:MandaloriansOnGalidraan-JFOS3.jpg]] |name=Mandalorian Civil War |begin=c. [[60 BBY/Legends|60 BBY]] |end=[[Battle of Galidraan]], [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]] |date= |place=Numerous, including [[Concord Dawn/Legends|Concord Dawn]], [[Korda Six]], and [[Galidraan/Legends|Galidraan]] |result=Defeat of the [[True Mandalorians]]; fragmentation of the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] |battles=*[[Battle of Concord Dawn]] *[[Battle of Korda Six]] *[[Battle of Galidraan]] |side1=[[True Mandalorians]] |side2=[[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=[[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mand'alor]] [[Jaster Mereel]] † Mand'alor [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] |commanders2=[[Secret Mandalore|Overlord]] [[Tor Vizsla]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] |commanders3= |commanders4= }} {{Quote|Now, Death Watch. Tried to oust Jaster Mereel because he liked law and order, and that crimped their game. Big turf war. [...] But you lot don't have a proper government like other species. You've got this loose arrangement of clans, and you've got a head of state who only shows up part-time and doesn't make the rules. How can the Death Watch overthrow anything? There's nothing to overthrow.''"
"''They can destroy our backbone.|[[Nyreen Vollen|Ny Vollen]] and [[Kal Skirata]]|''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''}} The '''Mandalorian Civil War''' was a conflict that erupted prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], following a schism in the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] clans that stemmed from a fundamental difference in ideology over the Mandalorians' place in the greater [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Fought between [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Jaster Mereel]]'s [[True Mandalorians]], who believed that the Mandalorians should act as honorable [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]], and [[Tor Vizsla]]'s splinter group known as [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]], which advocated that the Mandalorians return to their savage roots as raiders and brigands, the war lasted over a decade with victories and losses on both sides. Jaster Mereel would [[Death/Legends|fall]] on [[Korda Six]] as a result of a [[Montross (bounty hunter)|comrade's]] betrayal, while [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] succeeded him as the new ''Mand'alor'' and leader of the True Mandalorians until the devastating defeat at the [[Battle of Galidraan]] against the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. There, a Death Watch scheme unfolded that brought a contingent of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] led by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] under the false pretenses that the True Mandalorians were murdering civilians. When the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] attempted to take them into custody, fighting broke out that left eleven Jedi and every True Mandalorian on [[Galidraan/Legends|Galidraan]] dead, save for Jango Fett. ==History== ===Division=== In the year [[60 BBY/Legends|60 BBY]], a charismatic [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior by the name of [[Jaster Mereel]] ascended to the role of [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']], leader of all the Mandalorian clans.[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] In this role, he began to institute a cultural reform that many had been calling for since the [[Ithullan]] genocide nearly a century and a half earlier,{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}} eventually laying out a new guideline for Mandalorian behavior known as the [[Supercommando Codex]]. He asserted that the Mandalorians who wished to fight would simply be highly-paid soldiers, and should conduct themselves as honorable [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]].[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 1|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 1]] However, not all would agree with the Codex or Mereel's reforms. The [[New Mandalorians/Legends|New Mandalorians]]—a reformist political faction in existence following the aftermath of a devastating conflict with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] approximately [[738 BBY|678 years earlier]]—operated under a separate government out of their capital city [[Sundari/Legends|Sundari]], and believed in pacifism and non-violence above all.''[[The Essential Atlas]]''{{TCW|The Mandalore Plot}} More dangerously, though, were the followers of the barbaric Mandalorian soldier [[Tor Vizsla]], who dreamed of a goal opposing the Codex: a return to the ways of the [[Mandalorian Crusaders|ancient Mandalorians]] as raiders and conquerors, aiming to eventually wage a second [[Mandalorian Wars|war of conquest]] across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Skilled but undisciplined, these soldiers rallied to [[Tor Vizsla|Vizsla]] and formed a splinter group that came to call itself the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]]. The [[Mandalorian Supercommando|supercommandos]] loyal to [[Jaster Mereel]] recast themselves as the "[[True Mandalorians]]" and war soon broke out between the two factions. ===Early days of war=== {{Quote|No true Mandalorian can live alongside the Death Watch.|Kal Skirata|}} [[File:Vizsla.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Tor Vizsla, leader of the Death Watch]] One of the war's earliest battles occurred on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] agricultural [[Planet/Legends|world]], [[Concord Dawn/Legends|Concord Dawn]]. In [[58 BBY/Legends|58 BBY]], [[Battle of Concord Dawn|fighting]] broke out between the Death Watch and the True Mandalorians not far from [[Fett homestead|the farm]] belonging to the local [[Clan Fett|Fett family]]. After a firefight that turned in favor of the Death Watch, Jaster Mereel and his men fled into the fields of the Fett farm. While [[Fett (Elder Journeyman Protector)|the farm's owner]] offered the Mandalorians refuge, a vengeful Tor Vizsla tracked Mereel through the fields where he came upon the farmer's son, [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]]. Using the boy as leverage, Vizsla demanded the location of the True Mandalorians from his father, brutally beating the man when he refused to give up Mereel. When Vizsla threatened to kill the elder Fett in front of his son, [[Jango Fett's mother|Fett's wife]] attempted to save her husband, shooting and killing one of Vizsla's men with a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]]. The Death Watch struck back and in the fighting, both of young Jango's parents were killed, although Mereel and his troops were able to intervene and save Jango's life before retreating back into the field of crops. Vizsla ordered the fields be burned, attempting to kill Mereel as he ran, but as the fire spread all around them, Jango showed the True Mandalorians to an irrigation tube that led them to safety. His family gone—his parents dead and his sister, [[Arla Fett|Arla]], taken captive''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''—Jango chose to join the Mandalorians. [[File:Concorddawn.jpg|thumb|right|150px|True Mandalorian forces rescue a young Jango Fett from the Death Watch]] The Death Watch moved into a nearby town following their perceived victory over the True Mandalorians, intent on a celebratory raid before moving on to [[Moonus Mandel/Legends|Moonus Mandel]]. However, the True Mandalorians had regrouped in the town with the intent of staging a retaliatory strike. When Vizsla and his men arrived with their [[Death Watch tank|armored speeder]], Mereel's second-in-command [[Montross (bounty hunter)|Montross]] opened fire from a second-story window while the other True Mandalorians struck from protected alleys. Death Watch soldiers fell to [[Blaster/Legends|blasterfire]], while others died when Jango placed a [[Bomb/Legends|bomb]] on the underside of their speeder. While the True Mandalorians attempted to finish off their Death Watch foes, Jango confronted the man who'd murdered his parents, shooting him and avenging his dead mother and father. Nearly all of the Death Watch soldiers were killed in the firefight, although Tor Vizsla managed to somehow escape. As the True Mandalorians prepared to leave the planet, Jango was taken in by Mereel, who raised the boy as he would his own son. ===Return of the Death Watch=== {{Quote|It's the Death Watch!''"
"''Who?''"
"''Before your time, Silas. Ex-Mandalorians who split with Jaster years ago. We thought they were finished. Guess we were wrong.|Jango Fett and [[Silas (Mandalorian)|Silas]]|''[[Star Wars: Jango Fett: Open Seasons]]''}} [[File:Battle_of_Korda_6.jpg|thumb|left|250px|On Korda Six, the True Mandalorians open fire on the Death Watch ambush]] Despite Vizsla's escape, the True Mandalorians believed the Death Watch threat to be over in the wake of the events on Concord Dawn. Years later in [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]], the Death Watch would prove to still be very much active when they lured the True Mandalorians into a trap on the planet [[Korda Six]]. Hired by the [[Korda Defense Force]], the True Mandalorians journeyed to Korda Six to extract a team of rookie security personnel pinned down under fire by a group of local hostiles, supposedly poorly armed and with no formal army.[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 2]] When they arrived on the planet in several [[Meteor-class Q-Carrier|''Meteor''-class drop ships]], they found that the intelligence reports were grossly mistaken and were overwhelmed by superior [[Kordan]] forces even before the Death Watch revealed themselves. As Mereel gave the order to retreat, the Death Watch soldiers sprung their trap, attacking Jango Fett—now fourteen years old, and an adult in Mandalorian culture—and [[Jango's grunts|the squad]] under his command, while Jaster Mereel and Montross came under attack from Vizsla himself. Mereel was wounded in a [[Grenade/Legends|grenade]] blast that Montross used his [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]] to avoid, but when the injured ''Mand'alor'' called to him for an air lift, Montross turned his back on Mereel and left him to die on the battlefield, stating that he no longer wished to take orders from Mereel. Betrayed and abandoned, Mereel was helpless to defend himself and perished as Vizsla rained down [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] fire from his four-wheeled [[Tank/Legends|tank]]. [[File:Jaster_Mereel_death.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Jaster Mereel is gunned down by his enemy and rival, Tor Vizsla]] With Mereel dead, Montross returned to the True Mandalorians' landing zone and urged them to heed the ''Mand'alor''{{'s}} final orders and pull out, producing the false claim that not only was Mereel was dead, but that Jango had fallen trying to save him. However, Fett returned to the landing zone carrying with him Jaster Mereel's body, exposing Montross as a liar and a traitor. When Montross insisted that Mereel would have wanted him to take command, the other Mandalorians denounced Montross at blaster point and declared that they would only follow Jango, Jaster Mereel's chosen heir. Montross was grudgingly allowed to leave, slipping off into exile, while Jango became the new ''Mand'alor'' and leader of the True Mandalorians. ===War's end=== {{Quote|You were ''at'' Galidraan…tell me, how did one mere man kill so many of your kind?''"
"''With his bare hands.|[[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] and [[Dooku/Legends|Darth Tyranus]], speaking about Jango Fett|}} [[File:Jango_escape.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Jango Fett escapes from a Death Watch ambush on Galidraan]] In the years that followed the [[Battle of Korda Six]] and those leading up to [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]], the True Mandalorians scored a significant victory that left the Death Watch severely crippled. But rather than concede defeat, Vizsla turned to the [[Governor of Galidraan]] for refuge and his assistance in rebuilding the Death Watch. As per Vizsla's plan, the governor contracted the True Mandalorians to put down a local insurrection. The True Mandalorians traveled to [[Galidraan/Legends|Galidraan]] where they [[Battle of Galidraan|dealt with the rebels]], and upon completion of their task, Jango went to the governor's estate to collect their payment; Jango had also become aware of the governor's involvement with the Death Watch and while there, demanded to know the whereabouts of Vizsla. At that moment, Vizsla revealed himself there in the castle and attacked Jango with a cadre of Death Watch soldiers, forcing him to flee through a large window.[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 3|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 3]] By that time, however, the governor had already contacted the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] with a request for aid, claiming that the Mandalorians were murdering "political activists" along with innocent women and children. In response to the governor's concerns over the lack of evidence, Vizsla assured him that his own men would supply the necessary body count. [[File:GalidraanFaceoff-jfos.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Jango Fett stands among the bodies of the fallen True Mandalorians and slain Jedi.]] Jango rushed to make it back to the True Mandalorians' base camp, desperately trying without success to [[Comlink/Legends|comm]] his second-in-command, [[Myles]], and warn him of the inbound [[Consular-class light cruiser/Legends|Jedi ships]] he'd spotted, but he arrived at the same time as a Jedi strike team lead by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]]. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] had taken Galidraan's governor at his word and ordered the True Mandalorians to surrender on charges of suspected murder, with Dooku's [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Komari Vosa]] adding that should they resist, they would be killed. Innocent of the charges and indignant toward the Jedi, Jango ordered his troops to open fire, even giving specific orders to target Vosa. Their blasters quickly proved ineffective against the Jedi's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] and Jango called for the use of projectiles instead, and the provision of air cover via jetpacks. Myles attempted to provide air support for the group, but was [[Sai tok/Legends|killed]] in midair by one of the Jedi while Jango could only watch. During the battle, Jango personally killed six Jedi—including the one responsible for Myles' death—with no weapon beyond his own hands, feet, and armored body. But his actions were not enough to turn the tide: eleven Jedi lay dead at the battle's end, but [[Silas (Mandalorian)|Silas]] & Jango were the only True Mandalorian soldier there to survive the battle. Jango was finally subdued by the Jedi and delivered into the custody of Galidraan's governor, only to be stripped of his armor and sold into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] by the planet's ruler. [[File:Jangovsvizsla.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Jango fights Vizsla to the death]] After several years spent aboard a [[Transport/Legends|spice transport]] in bound servitude, Jango managed to escape, recover his [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]] from the Governor of Galidraan, and track [[Death Rattle|Vizsla's ship]] to [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] where he killed Vizsla after a lengthy fight, avenging the True Mandalorians and dealing the final blow of the war.[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 4|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 4]] Those Death Watch members that still remained scattered, disappearing from the galaxy at large until the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] where, now that the True Mandalorians were gone and Jango Fett dead, they turned their attention on the pacifistic New Mandalorians. ==Legacy and impact== {{Quote|Sectarian feud. ''Irrelevant'' to most ''Mando'ade's'' lives.|[[Jaing Skirata]]|''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''}} [[File:VizslaDeathWatchSymbol-OpenSeasons.png|thumb|right|110px|The Death Watch symbol]] [[File:MandoDaggerSkull.svg|thumb|left|125px|The True Mandalorian symbol]] The Mandalorian Civil War had been fought over ideology, how the Mandalorians would conduct themselves in the future, and poeticized by some as a battle for the very heart of the Mandalorians. Although the Death Watch succeeded in destroying their True Mandalorian foes, Jaster Mereel's Supercommando Codex would live on under his adopted son Jango's reign as ''Mand'alor'' and for generations after. In spite of its philosophical and ideological importance, the war had been fought between two relatively small factions, in what was seen by some as little more than a common power struggle. Mandalore's full-time army had been swallowed by the conflict, along with several prominent clans, but it had barely touched Mandalorians living off-world, even those in the [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]]. After the war, Jango Fett, scarred both by the loss of his True Mandalorian compatriots and his years as a slave, grew distant from the rest of his people and his role as ''Mand'alor'', turning instead toward the solitary life of a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] until his death at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]].{{Film|II}} ==Appearances== *[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']] {{Ab|Darth Plagueis (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 1|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 1]] {{1st}} {{Flash}} *[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2|''Jango
